context.description: A philosophical prose passage explains consciousness as the filter that turns sensory input into lived reality and the self. It uses the projection and light metaphor to contrast the machine-like body with awareness, then illustrates attention and memory through conscious breathing. A section heading introduces the idea that what the light illuminates becomes the world.
normalization_notes: Translated German prose into English control-language summary.
Mapped raw book/philosophical prose to consciousness-focused nonfiction philosophy.

rows.text_content: Awareness filters sensory events into reality and selfhood. Conscious breathing shows how attention creates memory and how control can be taken over but not deliberately released.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness book title Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2; page 40; section heading What the light illuminates is; philosophy of consciousness, awareness, perception, selfhood, attention, breathing, memory
